title: Gajan Sathananthan
content:
People.ContactEmail gsathananthan@mccarthy.ca
Gajan Sathananthan is a Partner in McCarthy Tétrault’s International Trade & Investment Law Group in Toronto. Known for his personable and practical approach, Gajan advises clients on a wide range of trade, regulatory, and compliance matters across many industries, including retail, agriculture, oil and gas, and industrial and consumer products.
Through his international trade practice, Gajan advises multinational and domestic businesses on how Canada’s laws related to trade with foreign countries may impact their business. Gajan’s deep understanding of market access issues including anti-dumping duties, tariffs and surcharges has helped clients develop and implement strategies to understand, comply with and mitigate the impact of these measures. He has also helped clients challenge the imposition of these measures, including before the Canadian International Trade Tribunal.
Gajan also has significant experience assisting clients with complex economic sanctions matters, including matters before the Federal Courts.
Clients appreciate his strategic advice on modern slavery issues, including Canada’s modern slavery reporting regime.

#### Regulatory and Compliance
Gajan’s regulatory practice is tailored to supporting clients at each stage of the regulatory continuum: from the earliest stages of product formulation and development, to packaging and labelling requirements, ensuring compliance with mandatory standards, obtaining requisite permits and licenses, and through to responding to regulatory enforcement actions.
Gajan also has significant experience in advertising matters, advising on marketing rules surrounding a variety of products, including regulated products such as cosmetics and natural health products. He has specific expertise with food regulatory issues, and has advised a number of clients on matters related to licensing and auditing under Canada’s food regulatory regime.
With a pulse on the latest international trade and regulatory developments, Gajan frequently writes timely thought leadership content with actionable insights to keep clients up to date and help them plan ahead.
Gajan received his LLB and BCL from McGill Law. He sits on the executive of the Ontario Bar Association’s International Law Section as its Director for Continued Professional Development programming, and is an active member of the Canadian Bar Association’s International Law Section and the American Bar Association’s International Law Section. He is a co-founder and acts as Treasurer of the Tamil Bar Association.
